beats.narration_text: Sawadee. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of infinite well superposition. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of infinite well superposition

beats.narration_text: Infinite Well Superposition is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. the core behaviour of infinite well superposition. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Infinite Well Superposition. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: c₁ (amplitude weight), Speed. Verify: the key behaviour of infinite well superposition. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Infinite Well Superposition. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: c₁ (amplitude weight), Speed. Verify: the k

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 02-infinite-well-superposition.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: infinite well superposition. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag c₁ (amplitude weight) from 0 to 1. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of c₁ (amplitude weight) from 0 to 1

beats.narration_text: the prediction holds — infinite well superposition behaves as the equations say. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of infinite well superposition. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Infinite Well Superposition simulation and add a second slider that controls c₁ (amplitude weight). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
